title: Individual widget state
body:
Right now, both instantsearch.js and instantsearch-react keep a shared helper state, that all components can connect to and refine.

However, this means that we cannot retrieve which user actions led to a given state. This is an issue for implementing the "Current Filters" widget (previously "currentRefinedValues"), for it won't display user friendly filter messages. Take a look at [this demo](http://codepen.io/team/algolia/pen/akaRyJ) and refine a few parameters. The filters are displayed one by one, as they are defined in the helper's state, and we loose track of which component defined which filter.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1621758/17363068/b0f8c432-5978-11e6-951c-abd2eb1e61c8.png)

On the other hand, it looks like SearchKit has per-component state. Take a look at [their demo](http://demo.searchkit.co/imdb?categories[0][0]=Movie&categories[1][0]=Drama&metascore[min]=27&metascore[max]=79&countries[0]=USA&runtimeMinutes[0]=61_1000): filters are linked to their widgets. The length filter even displays the label that was defined on the component.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1621758/17363264/a28ff2de-5979-11e6-88b4-ac44aafec35b.png)

If you look at the URL, you'll see that it is not the serialization of a shared state, but the serialization of each component's state separately.

In order for us to have a similar UX, we'd need to drop the shared helper's state and instead keep track of each component's state separately.

I believe that the current architecture can be modified to achieve this: the refine method of a connector's descriptor already takes in a "component state" argument and applies it to the shared state.
